description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray slate shingles, with a prominent stone chimney rising along one side. The upper story is constructed with timber framing and plaster infill, characteristic of traditional half-timbered architecture, while the lower story is built from rough-hewn stone blocks. Windows are varied in size and style, including a large bay window on the upper floor and smaller, multi-pane casement windows throughout. The structure includes visible wooden beams and decorative trusses, adding to its historic European appearance. The main entryway is set into the stone base, with a wooden door and small steps leading up to it. Overall, the house exudes a medieval or Tudor-era charm, suitable for a quaint urban neighborhood or historical district.
description: A two-story, half-timbered house with a steep gable roof, stone lower level, and prominent chimney, featuring varied windows and a rustic medieval appearance.
description_view_0: The house is seen from the side, highlighting the slate-tiled roof, stone chimney, and upper timber-framed story with two windows.
description_view_1: This end view shows the steep gable roof, large upper bay window, decorative timber trusses, and a small door at the stone base.
description_view_2: The opposite side reveals the roof, a central bay window on the upper floor, and two shuttered windows on the stone lower level.
description_view_3: The other gable end displays a large arched window, timber framing, and the chimney set behind the roof ridge.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with bay and arched windows
attribute_entryway_design: Stone base entry with wooden door, small steps, and minimal decorative elements
